analysis.test: To cumulate an action for parasitism (unfair free-riding) (art. 1240 C. civ.) with an action for copyright infringement (art. L122-4 CPI), the facts underlying the parasitism claim must be DISTINCT from those underlying the infringement claim: additional conduct, not covered by the mere unlawful reproduction/public performance of Quiz Mania, must evidence an intent to ride on the coattails of Méridian Studios by saving identifiable investments. Absent such distinction, the two causes of action tend toward the same […] ends and their cumulation is inadmissible (Cass. com., 18 mars 2026, n°24-17.016 [1]).
faits: Méridian Studios developed and exploited Bastille through its own independent, documented investments: prototype development, design, attested expenditures (Exhibit 13, accountant's attestation), and trademark registration Bastille n°4449595 (Exhibit 2) — all assets distinct from any reproduction of a work belonging to Cassiopée.

The sales recorded on Amazon (Exhibit 6 bis), the […] coverage (Exhibits 18.1 tweet Manon Girard 2, 18.6, 18.8, 3 bis) and the LSA article (Exhibit 17) attest to a commercial exploitation specific to Méridian Studios, built on its own investments […], not reducible to the sole alleged reproduction of a work.

The judgment of the TJ Paris of 18 December 2020 (Exhibit 12, adverse party) itself distinguished the acts of copyright infringement from unfair commercial practices (offering a gift in exchange for a […] review), confirming that distinct grievances may coexist — but that Cassiopée must identify them precisely and separately.

Cassiopée produces no exhibit quantifying the cost of its specific investments (development, promotion, communications) nor demonstrating that Méridian Studios saved such costs through conduct distinct from the alleged reproduction of Quiz Mania.

Cassiopée's message to its distributor (Exhibit 10) and the letter of 5 October 2021 (Exhibit 5, adverse party) reveal that Cassiopée bases its grievances on the similarity of the games, without identifying any autonomous act of parasitism (e.g., solicitation of partners, copying of marketing materials, diversion of customers) distinct from the alleged reproduction.
analysis.faille: The […] listing on Leboncoin (bailiff's report dated 02/10/2019) — seeking the game 'Carto Royale 4 […] before 2019' — could be presented by Cassiopée as a distinct act of parasitism (unfair free-riding) (attempt to appropriate the reputation of Carto Royale 4 beyond the mere reproduction of Tohu-Bohu). Méridian Studios has not yet provided documented explanation of the origin and purpose of that listing.
analysis.riposte: The Leboncoin listing predates any litigation and is consistent with a routine market-monitoring or market-research exercise, bearing no connection to any identifiable savings on Cassiopée's investments. Moreover, Cassiopée quantifies no specific investment from which Méridian Studios allegedly profited through that listing: a parasitism claim requires proof of an individualized economic value and of a saving actually realized (Cass. com., 26 juin 2024, n°22-17.647 [3]). Finally, if Cassiopée is unable to identify a distinct act in its pleadings, the cumulation must be declared inadmissible (Cass. com., 18 mars 2026, n°24-17.016 [1]).

autorites.title: Cass. com. 24-17.016, 2026-03-18
themes: […] formée pour la […] fois en appel
autorites.summary: Lorsqu'elles reposent sur les […] faits, une action en contrefaçon et une action en concurrence déloyale ou fondée sur le parasitisme tendent aux […] fins, à savoir l'interdiction de fabrication et de commercialisation d'un produit ou d'un […] et la réparation du préjudice subi du fait de cette commercialisation. Il s'ensuit que la partie […] a introduit, en […] instance, une action en contrefaçon rejetée pour défaut de droit privatif est recevable à former, pour la […] fois en appel, une […] en concurrence déloyale ou fondée sur le parasitisme, lorsque ces […] reposent sur les […] faits

autorites.title: Cass. com. 22-17.647, 2024-06-26
autorites.summary:
Le parasitisme économique est une forme de déloyauté, constitutive d'une faute au sens de l'article 1240 du code civil, […] consiste, pour un opérateur économique, à se placer dans le sillage d'un autre afin de tirer indûment profit de ses efforts, de son savoir-faire, de la notoriété acquise ou des investissements consentis. 
Il appartient à celui […] se prétend victime d'actes de parasitisme d'identifier la valeur économique individualisée qu'il invoque, ainsi que la volonté d'un tiers de se placer dans son sillage. 
Le savoir-faire et les efforts humains et financiers propres à caractériser une valeur économique identifiée et individualisée ne peuvent se déduire de la seule longévité et du succès de la commercialisation du produit et, les idées étant de […] parcours, le seul fait de reprendre, en le déclinant, un concept mis en oeuvre par un concurrent ne constitue pas, en soi, un acte de parasitisme. 
Doit être approuvée la cour d'appel ayant retenu le grief de parasitisme après avoir retenu, d'une part qu'était démontrée la valeur économique identifiée et individualisée du produit invoqué comme ayant été parasité, caractérisée par sa grande notoriété, la réalité du travail de conception et de développement, le caractère innovant de la démarche conduite, ainsi que les investissements publicitaires, d'autre part la commercialisation d'un produit identique d'un point de vue fonctionnel et fortement inspiré de l'apparence du produit invoqué, par un concurrent ne justifiant d'aucun travail de mise au point ni de coût exposés relatifs à son produit, dès lors que cette commercialisation est intervenue à une période au cours de laquelle la société […] investissait encore pour la promotion de son produit, devenu phare et connu d'une large partie du grand public grâce aux lourds investissements publicitaires consentis depuis plusieurs […], […] il n'était pas établi ni même allégué que des articles équivalents auraient existé sur le marché au moment de son lancement, l'ensemble de ces éléments démontrant la volonté de la société concurrente de se placer dans le sillage d'autrui pour bénéficier du succès rencontré auprès de la clientèle par le produit et, sans aucune contrepartie ni prise de risque, d'un avantage concurrentiel

analysis.acte_referme: A witness statement or internal memorandum from Méridian Studios explaining the purpose of the Leboncoin listing (e.g., market research, competitive intelligence, purchase of second-hand games for testing), accompanied by a bailiff's report or screenshot showing that the listing predates or is unrelated to the commercial exploitation of Bastille — to be […] with an analysis of the adverse party's pleadings showing that Cassiopée targets only the similarity of the games in its […] claims for unfair competition.
analysis.moyen_adverse: Cassiopée argues that the Leboncoin listing (bailiff's report of 02/10/2019) constitutes a distinct act of parasitism (free-riding) — Méridian Studios allegedly sought to misappropriate the goodwill and know-how of Carto Royale 4 by specifically targeting […] predating 2019, which […] goes beyond the mere reproduction of Tohu-Bohu and evidences an independent intent to ride on Cassiopée's coattails.

statut_preuve.pourquoi: Méridian Studios has solid exhibits regarding its own investments (Exhibits 2, 13, 17, 18.x), but the demonstration that Cassiopée's unfair competition claim is based on facts DISTINCT from the copyright infringement rests on an analysis of the adverse party's pleadings rather than on positive exhibits specific to Méridian Studios. The risk is that Cassiopée identifies, at the last moment, a distinct fact (e.g., a Leboncoin advertisement seeking the game 'Carto Royale 4 […] before 2019') in order to preserve the cumulation of claims.

analysis.these_adverse: Cassiopée argues, relying on the judgment of the TJ Paris of 18 December 2020 (Exhibit 12) and on the decision of the TGI Paris of 4 April 2014 (Exhibit 11), that distinct acts of unfair competition may coexist with a copyright infringement action provided they are precisely identified — in the present case, the Leboncoin listing targeting 'Carto Royale 4 […] before 2019' and the message to the distributor (Exhibit 10) would constitute autonomous acts of parasitism (unfair free-riding), distinct from the reproduction of Tohu-Bohu. Cassiopée further argues, relying on Cass. com. du 9 juin 2021 (Exhibit 25), that disloyal conduct by M. [A] [L] is established independently of the infringement, which […] would justify cumulation. However, Cassiopée does not quantify any investment of its own whose cost Méridian Studios allegedly saved, which […] deprives its parasitism claim of one of its essential constituent elements.

pieces_adverses.piece: Bailiff's report (constat d'huissier) of 02/10/2019 concerning the Leboncoin listing 'Carto Royale 4 […] before 2019'
pieces_adverses.contre: The report establishes the existence of the advertisement but not its author or any parasitic purpose; Méridian Studios may demonstrate that it constitutes a competitive-intelligence exercise unconnected to any investment by Cassiopée

pieces_adverses.piece: Message from Cassiopée to its distributor (Exhibit 10) presented as evidence of a distinct act of customer diversion
pieces_adverses.contre: This message originates from Cassiopée itself and does not prove any act by Méridian Studios; it does not establish an autonomous parasitic course of conduct by Méridian Studios distinct from the alleged reproduction

pieces_adverses.piece: Decision TGI Paris, 4 avril 2014 (Exhibit 11) invoked to justify the cumulation of copyright infringement and unfair competition claims
pieces_adverses.contre: This decision concerns a different sector ([…]/lingerie) and clearly identified distinct facts; in the present case Cassiopée does not establish a quantified autonomous act, which […] distinguishes the two situations

pieces_attendues: Internal memorandum or email exchange from Méridian Studios explaining the purpose of the Leboncoin listing (market monitoring, product testing)
Itemized schedule of Méridian Studios' own investments (development, marketing, promotion) with supporting accounting records supplementing Exhibit 13
Comparative analysis of the adverse party's pleadings showing that Cassiopée targets only the similarity of the games in its […] unfair competition claims (without identifying any distinct fact)
Process server's report [constat d'huissier] or timestamped screenshot of the Leboncoin listing with contextual information (date, author profile, absence of any connection to Cassiopée)
